name: RangeClarity Kanban Board
source_of_truth: Linear when KANBAN_PROVIDER=linear and credentials are configured; otherwise this file plus docs/kanban-board.md.

milestones.title: Domain live
milestones.done_condition: RangeClarity.com resolves to the intended landing page with HTTPS.

milestones.title: Landing page live
milestones.done_condition: Premium meme-fintech TradingView landing page is live, mobile-safe, and CTA-ready.

milestones.title: Waitlist or early-access capture live
milestones.done_condition: Visitor can take one clear action to join early access or request Pro.

milestones.title: TradingView setup page live
milestones.done_condition: Setup page explains Starter to waitlist/Discord to invite-only Pro flow.

milestones.title: Starter / Range Map MVP built and tested
milestones.done_condition: TradingView Starter scope is implemented and tested on realistic chart scenarios.

milestones.title: 10 early testers recruited
milestones.done_condition: 10 TradingView users agree to test and give structured feedback.

milestones.title: 3 users willing to pay
milestones.done_condition: 3 qualified users explicitly say they would pay for Pro access.

milestones.title: First paid Pro plan launched
milestones.done_condition: Manual or Whop-backed paid access flow is live for first customers.

milestones.title: 10 paying users
milestones.done_condition: 10 active paid users are onboarded without breaking support flow.

milestones.title: 50 paying users
milestones.done_condition: Repeatable acquisition, onboarding, and support loop supports 50 paid users.

milestones.title: Weekly content engine running
milestones.done_condition: Weekly chart-context content cadence runs for 4 consecutive weeks.

tasks.title: Choose the first conversion path
tasks.description: Decide whether early access routes through a waitlist form, Discord invite, Whop page, or manual Telegram/DM capture.
acceptance_criteria: One primary conversion path is selected.
The decision includes where CTA traffic should land.
The path avoids backend, auth, or payment complexity unless approved.
tasks.definition_of_done: Landing page CTA work can proceed without guessing the destination.

tasks.title: Make landing page CTA concrete
tasks.description: Wire the primary and secondary CTAs to the chosen early-access path.
acceptance_criteria: Primary CTA has a clear destination.
Secondary CTA supports setup or sample Range Map viewing.
CTA copy stays no-hype and TradingView-first.
tasks.definition_of_done: A visitor can take the intended early-access action from the landing page.

tasks.title: Final landing page trust pass
tasks.description: Add or tighten FAQ, disclaimer, and no-hype language across the page.
acceptance_criteria: No copy promises trades, win rate, profit, or personalized advice.
FAQ and disclaimer are visible.
TradingView indicator-suite positioning remains premium and clear.
tasks.definition_of_done: The page feels funny but serious, credible, and compliant enough for early testers.

tasks.title: Freeze Starter / Range Map MVP spec
tasks.description: Define exactly what the Starter indicator includes and excludes.
acceptance_criteria: Included signals and visuals are listed.
Excluded Pro features are named.
At least 5 test scenarios are listed.
Compliance language is included.
tasks.definition_of_done: The MVP is build-ready without inviting scope creep.

tasks.title: Draft early tester outreach message
tasks.description: Write a short outreach message for recruiting TradingView users into the first tester group.
acceptance_criteria: Message explains RangeClarity as chart context, not signals.
Message asks for feedback rather than promising outcomes.
Message includes the next action for the tester.
tasks.definition_of_done: Founder can send the message to 10 candidate testers.

tasks.title: Preserve premium meme-fintech landing identity
tasks.description: Keep the current RangeClarity landing direction coherent while future edits happen.
acceptance_criteria: Dark market-command-center visual direction remains intact.
TradingView toolkit and collectible module cards remain obvious.
The page does not drift into generic SaaS or scammy signal-bot framing.
tasks.definition_of_done: Future edits can point to the design system and current implementation as the standard.

tasks.title: Keep Hermes RangeClarity command center working
tasks.description: Maintain Telegram commands for project status, plan, weekly priorities, Kanban, and Linear planning.
acceptance_criteria: Hermes can report RangeClarity status.
Hermes can draft Linear-ready tasks without creating real issues by default.
Hermes does not expose secrets.
tasks.definition_of_done: Founder can use Hermes as the RangeClarity execution manager from Telegram.

tasks.title: Publish domain and HTTPS checklist
tasks.description: Document DNS, hosting, HTTPS, canonical URL, and smoke-test steps.
acceptance_criteria: Checklist names the DNS and hosting actions.
HTTPS and canonical URL are included.
Smoke test steps are included.
tasks.definition_of_done: Founder can make RangeClarity.com live without missing the basic launch checks.

tasks.title: Build TradingView setup page content
tasks.description: Create setup flow content for discovery, install, early access, invite approval, and support.
acceptance_criteria: Starter discovery flow is described.
Pro invite-only flow is described.
Support and limitation language are clear.
tasks.definition_of_done: Setup page content is ready to implement on the site.

tasks.title: Write Range Map test scenarios
tasks.description: Write scenario notes for range, support/resistance, compression, breakout watch, and late-entry risk.
acceptance_criteria: At least 5 chart scenarios are documented.
Each scenario states expected indicator behavior.
The scenarios distinguish context from trade recommendations.
tasks.definition_of_done: Starter / Range Map MVP has practical validation cases.

tasks.title: Create tester feedback form/questions
tasks.description: Capture clarity, confusion, trust, willingness to pay, and feature objections from early testers.
acceptance_criteria: Questions cover value, trust, confusion, and pricing.
Questions do not ask for trading outcomes.
Responses can be grouped into product and GTM decisions.

tasks.title: Run 3 willingness-to-pay conversations
tasks.description: Talk to 3 qualified users about pricing and perceived value.
acceptance_criteria: 3 qualified users give pricing/value feedback.
Notes capture objections and willingness to pay.
Conversation avoids signal/profit framing.
tasks.definition_of_done: Founder has a real demand signal before paid launch decisions.

tasks.title: Decide launch pricing
tasks.description: Pick the initial Pro price and annual/monthly framing.
acceptance_criteria: Initial Pro monthly price is chosen.
Annual option is either chosen or intentionally deferred.
Pricing copy does not imply trading results.
tasks.definition_of_done: Landing and checkout/waitlist copy can use one clear pricing decision.

tasks.title: Choose first access/payment mechanism
tasks.description: Choose manual access, Whop, or waitlist-only prelaunch path for the first paid Pro flow.
acceptance_criteria: One launch mechanism is selected.
Support and invite handling are considered.
No backend is added unless explicitly approved.
tasks.definition_of_done: First paid access path can be implemented or documented.

tasks.title: Create weekly chart-context content backlog
tasks.description: Create 12 educational post ideas around range, trend, support/resistance, extension, and risk.
acceptance_criteria: 12 content ideas are listed.
Every idea teaches context instead of calls.
Ideas can be posted on TradingView, X, or Discord.
tasks.definition_of_done: The founder has a month of weekly content options.

tasks.title: Define onboarding checklist for paid Pro users
tasks.description: Clarify how a paid user gets access, installs indicators, gets support, and understands limitations.
acceptance_criteria: Access, install, support, and limitation steps are listed.
Checklist avoids personalized trading advice.
Checklist can be reused in Discord or email.
tasks.definition_of_done: First paid users can onboard without founder improvising every step.

tasks.title: Track early objections and product risks
tasks.description: Group early objections into copy, product, pricing, trust, and onboarding buckets.
acceptance_criteria: Objection buckets are defined.
Each tester note can map to at least one bucket.
Founder can see what needs fixing before paid launch.
tasks.definition_of_done: Feedback creates action instead of a pile of vague notes.

done.title: Add Hermes dry-run Linear planner
done.done_evidence: Hermes can draft Linear tickets in dry-run mode and requires approval before creation.

done.title: Add Telegram setup/status checks
done.done_evidence: RangeClarity setup test, /rcsetup, and /rcstatus return deterministic project context.
